findings.title: --filter is interpolated into a shell command
findings.impact: When dscli top is invoked with a filter containing shell metacharacters or quotes, that text is embedded directly in a shell pipeline and then executed with shell=True. Any local caller, script, or higher-level tool that forwards filter text can run arbitrary commands as the dscli user instead of only narrowing the worker list.
findings.fix: Avoid shell=True and pass argv lists to subprocess, e.g. run pgrep with ["pgrep", "-fa", "--", _WORKER_ADDRESS_ARG], then apply the optional substring filter in Python; alternatively pass grep arguments as an argv list without a shell.
